strategy: ⚠️ OBSERVE — Heavy Insider Selling; Enter Only Post-Earnings Stabilization

wheelStrategy.ineligibleReason: Stock price $119.00 — 1 CSP contract requires ~$11,900 cash. Marginally above $10,000 threshold, and heavy insider selling (Magnetar distribution) makes assignment risk unacceptable. Use Calls/Spreads only.
wheelStrategy.wheelFlag: WHEEL-UNSUITABLE: Heavy institutional selling — Magnetar sold 209K shares + covered calls capping upside at $150. CEO-linked entities sold $124M+.
wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. Wait for VIX > 20 before entering premium-selling positions.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: N/A — Wheel not applicable for this stock

exitRules.entryTrigger: Enter post-Q1 2026 earnings stabilization. Stock at $113.80 — $6.20 below $120 buy leg. Enter small (1 spread max, 1% portfolio). Wait for insider selling pressure to ease before sizing up.
exitRules.profitExit: Close spread at 70–80% of max gain. First target: stock at $135–140 for ~$15 spread value (+70% on debit). Second target: $145+ for full $25 max gain (~140% on Nov debit).
exitRules.timeStop: If stock has not cleared $125 by ~July 1 (60% of time elapsed), close. Heavy insider selling creates overhead resistance.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 50% loss on net debit. CRWV has heavy institutional selling — do not average down.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close by August 13 (5 days before August 18 Q2 earnings). IV at 80% means massive IV crush risk — exit well before earnings.

options.bestFor: Unusable — Aug 15 expiry is only 2 days after the Aug 13 mandatory close. You must exit before the option expires, leaving essentially zero remaining time value.
Amber zone — 37 days at close is workable but suboptimal. Time value is meaningful but IV crush risk is high. Better than Aug 15, but Nov 21 is preferred for this earnings-driven stock.

Recommended — 100 days of time value at the August 13 close deadline. Gives CRWV time to recover from insider selling pressure and re-rate on Anthropic/Microsoft contract news. Spread structure keeps debit manageable despite 80% IV.

recommendation.title: Bull Call Spread — But HEAVY INSIDER SELLING is a Red Flag; Small Size Only
recommendation.entryNote: ⚠️ RED FLAG — INSTITUTIONAL INSIDER SELLING: Magnetar funds (major pre-IPO investor) sold 209,157 shares at $119–121 AND sold calls on 1,000,000 and 2,000,000 shares at $150–160 strikes (Dec 2026 expiry) — they are CAPPING UPSIDE while selling stock. CEO-linked entities (Venturo Family Trust + West Clay Capital) sold 1,125,000 shares at $110–118. COO also sold. This is major institutional distribution. HIGH IV (~75–100%) mandates spread. Stock at $119, 52-week range $49–$187 (-36% from ATH). Revenue is explosive (+168% to $5.13B in 2025) but company is still unprofitable (-$1.2B). Citi PT $155 is the bull case. Q1 2026 earnings likely just reported (May 2026) — if not yet, wait. Enter 1 spread ONLY, 1% portfolio max. This is not a high-conviction entry.

exitPlan.label: Spread 1
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $135–140 (+13–17%) on Anthropic or Meta contract expansion news

exitPlan.label: Spread 2
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $145+ (+22%) on path-to-profitability clarity or new hyperscaler deal

companyOverview: CoreWeave is the largest independent AI-native GPU cloud provider, IPO'd in early 2025 on NASDAQ. It rents out NVIDIA GPU compute (primarily H100/H200/GB200 clusters) to AI companies that need burst capacity without owning hardware. Revenue surged 168% YoY to $5.13B in 2025 as AI training and inference demand exploded — but the company remains deeply unprofitable (-$1.2B net loss). Key partnerships: multi-year agreement with Anthropic to power Claude AI models, expanded partnership with Meta Platforms for AI compute. The stock hit ATH of $187 in late 2025 / early 2026 and has pulled back 36% to $119. The fundamental bull case is simple: AI labs need more compute than they can buy; CoreWeave fills that gap. The risk: Magnetar (major pre-IPO investor) is aggressively distributing shares and SELLING CALL OPTIONS to cap upside — the single most bearish insider signal on this list.

financials.note: -36% from ATH — meaningful pullback but not extreme discount
+27.6% more losses than 2024 — growing faster than losses, but not profitable
Extremely high beta — 3x more volatile than S&P 500
Recent IPO with very high beta — spread MANDATORY
Both are multi-year commitments; revenue visibility positive

insiderFlow.summary: 🔴 HEAVY SELLING — one of the most bearish insider flow patterns on this list. Magnetar (pre-IPO investor) is both SELLING STOCK and SELLING CALL OPTIONS to cap upside. CEO-linked entities sold 1.125M shares. COO sold. This is institutional distribution.

transactions.note: 🔴 MOST BEARISH: Sold 209K shares AND sold call options on 1M–2M shares at $150–160 strikes (Dec 2026). Selling calls = Magnetar does NOT expect stock to reach $150 by year-end. This caps upside.

transactions.insider: Venturo Family Trust + West Clay Capital LLC (CEO-linked)
transactions.note: 🔴 CEO-linked entities converted Class B→Class A and immediately sold 1.125M shares. Massive distribution at $110–118.
Pre-planned 10b5-1 sale. Additional executive selling layer.

insiderFlow.signal: 🔴 RED FLAG — Do NOT ignore this. Magnetar selling covered calls at $150–160 explicitly signals they don't believe in upside above $150. CEO-linked entities selling $124M+ of stock right after IPO lock-up. Heavy distribution across multiple insiders. Bull thesis requires believing the fundamentals will override this selling pressure.

catalysts: Anthropic multi-year compute contract — powers Claude AI models; Anthropic is one of the fastest-growing AI companies
Meta expanded partnership — Meta's AI compute needs are enormous; CRWV is a key alternative to internal infra
Revenue +168% YoY growth — if this rate sustains even at 80% in 2026, revenue hits $9B+
Citi raised PT to $155 — institutional conviction despite insider selling; suggests sell-side sees path to profitability
AI compute structural shortage — demand for GPU cloud exceeds supply; CRWV benefits from this scarcity premium
risks: 🔴 MAGNETAR SELLING COVERED CALLS at $150–160: this explicitly signals pre-IPO investors do NOT expect >$150 in 2026. Treat as a hard ceiling unless fundamentals dramatically improve.
🔴 CEO-linked entities sold $124M+ of stock: this is not tax withholding — it is portfolio liquidation at post-IPO unlock
Still unprofitable (-$1.2B in 2025); path to profitability unclear; high debt from GPU leasing agreements
High IV (~75–100%): spreads are expensive; max gain is capped; premium decay is fast if stock doesn't move
Beta 3.27: any broad market selloff hits CRWV 3x harder; not defensive at all
NVIDIA dependency: entire business depends on NVIDIA GPU supply; any allocation shift or price increase directly impacts margins
Lock-up expirations: post-IPO lock-ups create ongoing selling overhang as more pre-IPO investors become eligible to sell

dilution.summary: HIGH DILUTION RISK. Recent IPO means multiple rounds of lock-up expirations are still ahead. Debt financing for GPU infrastructure is capital-intensive.

items.label: Post-IPO lock-up expirations
items.detail: Multiple insider/pre-IPO lock-up tranches expire throughout 2026. Each expiration creates fresh selling supply.

items.label: GPU infrastructure debt
items.detail: CoreWeave finances GPU fleets through debt. As fleets age, refresh cycles require new capital raises.

items.label: Ongoing equity offerings
items.detail: At -$1.2B annual net loss, CoreWeave likely needs additional capital. Secondary offerings are probable in 2026–2027.
